comment_text.sn10.8:1.1: This story, which tells of the conversion of the Buddha’s chief layman follower, is detailed further in the Vinaya at Kd 16:4.1.1. | The Cool Grove was a pleasant place to meditate, notable as the place where the Buddha moderated the monk Soṇa’s excessive striving (AN 6.55).
comment_text.sn10.8:1.2: His given name was Sudatta, but he is usually known by the sobriquet Anāthapiṇḍika, “feeder of the poor”. He features in countless discourses as the ideal of the generous lay follower.
comment_text.sn10.8:1.12: Sivaka appears alongside Mucalinda at DN 32:10.12. Mucalinda supported the Buddha after his awakening, as Sivaka supports Anāthapiṇḍika here. Sivaka might be identified with Shiva (see SN 2.21).
comment_text.sn10.8:2.1: The commentary says that the “hundred” in the first two lines should be multiplied by the “thousand” of line three, but the Chinese parallels at SA 592 and SA2 186 prefer the more modest figure.
comment_text.sn10.8:11.7: After all that drama, he asks a simple question, as one does when nervous. But the Buddha’s reply is more than satisfying.

root_text.sn10.8:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati sītavane.
root_text.sn10.8:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati rājagahaṁ anuppatto hoti kenacideva karaṇīyena.
root_text.sn10.8:1.5: Tāvadeva ca pana bhagavantaṁ dassanāya upasaṅkamitukāmo hoti. Athassa anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa etadahosi:
root_text.sn10.8:1.7: Sve dānāhaṁ kālena bhagavantaṁ dassanāya gamissāmī”ti buddhagatāya satiyā nipajji.
root_text.sn10.8:1.8: Rattiyā sudaṁ tikkhattuṁ vuṭṭhāsi pabhātanti maññamāno.
root_text.sn10.8:1.9: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena sivathikadvāraṁ tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.sn10.8:1.11: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa nagaramhā nikkhamantassa āloko antaradhāyi, andhakāro pāturahosi, bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁso udapādi, tatova puna nivattitukāmo ahosi.
root_text.sn10.8:1.12: Atha kho sivako yakkho antarahito saddamanussāvesi:
root_text.sn10.8:4.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa andhakāro antaradhāyi, āloko pāturahosi, yaṁ ahosi bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁso, so paṭippassambhi.
root_text.sn10.8:4.2: Dutiyampi kho anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa āloko antaradhāyi, andhakāro pāturahosi, bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁso udapādi, tatova puna nivattitukāmo ahosi.
root_text.sn10.8:4.3: Dutiyampi kho sivako yakkho antarahito saddamanussāvesi:
root_text.sn10.8:7.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa andhakāro antaradhāyi, āloko pāturahosi, yaṁ ahosi bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁso, so paṭippassambhi.
root_text.sn10.8:7.2: Tatiyampi kho anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa āloko antaradhāyi, andhakāro pāturahosi, bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁso udapādi, tatova puna nivattitukāmo ahosi.
root_text.sn10.8:7.3: Tatiyampi kho sivako yakkho antarahito saddamanussāvesi:
root_text.sn10.8:10.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa andhakāro antaradhāyi, āloko pāturahosi, yaṁ ahosi bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁso, so paṭippassambhi.
root_text.sn10.8:10.2: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena sītavanaṁ yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.sn10.8:11.1: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya abbhokāse caṅkamati.
root_text.sn10.8:11.2: Addasā kho bhagavā anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.sn10.8:11.3: Disvāna caṅkamā orohitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn10.8:11.4: Nisajja kho bhagavā anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn10.8:11.6: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati, nāmena maṁ bhagavā ālapatīti, haṭṭho udaggo tattheva bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:

translation_text.sn10.8:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha in the Cool Grove.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.2: Now at that time the householder Anāthapiṇḍika had arrived at Rājagaha on some business.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.4: a rumor that a Buddha had arisen in the world.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.5: Right away he wanted to go and see the Buddha, but he thought,
translation_text.sn10.8:1.6: “It’s too late to go and see the Buddha today.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.7: I’ll go and see him tomorrow.” He went to bed thinking of the Buddha.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.8: During the night he got up three times thinking it was morning.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.9: Then he approached the Sivaka Gate,
translation_text.sn10.8:1.10: and non-human beings opened it for him.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.11: But as he was leaving the city, light vanished and darkness appeared to him. He felt fear, terror, and goosebumps, and wanted to turn back.
translation_text.sn10.8:1.12: Then the invisible spirit Sivaka called out:
translation_text.sn10.8:2.5: these are not worth a sixteenth part
translation_text.sn10.8:3.3: Going forward is better for you,
translation_text.sn10.8:4.1: Then darkness vanished and light appeared to Anāthapiṇḍika. His fear, terror, and goosebumps settled down.
translation_text.sn10.8:4.2: But for a second time, light vanished and darkness appeared to him. …
translation_text.sn10.8:4.3: For a second time the invisible spirit Sivaka called out …
translation_text.sn10.8:6.3: “… Going forward is better for you,
translation_text.sn10.8:7.1: Then darkness vanished and light appeared to Anāthapiṇḍika. His fear, terror, and goosebumps settled down.
translation_text.sn10.8:7.2: But for a third time, light vanished and darkness appeared to him. …
translation_text.sn10.8:7.3: For a third time the invisible spirit Sivaka called out …
translation_text.sn10.8:9.3: “… Going forward is better for you,
translation_text.sn10.8:10.1: Then darkness vanished and light appeared to Anāthapiṇḍika. His fear, terror, and goosebumps settled down.
translation_text.sn10.8:10.2: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went to the Cool Grove and approached the Buddha.
translation_text.sn10.8:11.1: Now at that time the Buddha had risen at the crack of dawn and was walking mindfully in the open.
translation_text.sn10.8:11.2: He saw Anāthapiṇḍika coming off in the distance.
translation_text.sn10.8:11.3: So he stepped down from the walking path, sat down on the seat spread out,
translation_text.sn10.8:11.6: Then Anāthapiṇḍika thought, “The Buddha calls me by name!” Smiling and elated, he bowed with his head at the Buddha’s feet and said to him,
translation_text.sn10.8:11.7: “Sir, I trust the Buddha slept well?”
translation_text.sn10.8:13.1: Since they’ve cut off all clinging,
translation_text.sn10.8:13.2: and removed the stress from the heart,
